source_name: Code4rena Ethena Labs Findings and Analysis Report (2024-11) - Low Risk and Non-Critical Issues
issue_claim: The redistributeLockedAmount() function does not verify if the address to possesses the WHITELISTED_ROLE when WHITELIST_ENABLED.
why_it_looked_bad: When the state is WHITELIST_ENABLED, only addresses with the WHITELISTED_ROLE are permitted to receive UStb. However, in the redistributeLockedAmount() function, the only check performed is to ensure that the address to does not have the BLACKLISTED_ROLE. Consequently, redistribution to an unwhitelisted address is allowed.
why_not_valid: redistribution whitelist omission matters under admin-controlled redistribution assumptions and lacks a standalone permissionless exploit path
downgrade_reason: redistribution whitelist omission matters under admin-controlled redistribution assumptions and lacks a standalone permissionless exploit path
trust_assumption: Treat as a caution/false-positive reference unless the current target shows permissionless reachability, direct value loss, and concrete affected code path.
when_it_could_be_real: if combined with permissionless access or large-value state transitions
if the affected path becomes critical to mint/redeem/withdrawal solvency or availability
